column3: item
Removed the 'Rev/chip decline mult. to 2031' hack entirely - no deflation factor baked into 2031 revenue (per user).
Removed standalone 'duty cycle' parameter; folded idle+batching into one 'effective concurrency' to cut an assumption.
Replaced ambiguous 'serving share' with an explicit 'inference fraction (20-50%)' parameter used consistently across methods.
Eliminated the separate 'Inputs summary' sheet; single Raw-data source + a clearly-labelled derived layer.
Re-derived Anthropic revenue-per-model from ARR/fleet instead of a mysterious primitive; deleted the confusing 'collapse M1/M2' note.
Made parameters.csv the single source of truth - directly editable in VSCode; drives both the Quarto report and the Excel.
Adopted a flat, uniform parameter schema: id, group, value, low, high, dist, unit, source, url, note.
Grouped parameters logically (Hardware, Model, Throughput, Pricing, Utilisation, Revenue, Compute, Epoch, Scale) for consistent organisation.
Integrated the OpenRouter token method as a first-class method pulling concurrency from raw data.
Concurrency parameter note lists the three calibration data points (Epoch-implied ~3, FLOP-implied ~32, production batch 8-128).
Set inference fraction distribution to Triangular(0.20, 0.35, 0.50) to honour the requested 20-50% range with a central mode.
Recognised GPU-DeepSeek and Token-OpenRouter are the same calculation in two framings; will present once with both expressions to avoid redundant complexity.
Kept GPU-macro method applied to ALL GPUs (no inference fraction) because $5k is already a fleet-wide average - avoids double-counting.
Applied inference fraction to the Epoch token-capacity method (Epoch's figure assumes full-fleet inference, so the fraction is not double-counted).
H100 peak compute (FP8, dense): value=1979000000000000.0 unit=FLOP/s dist=point - sourced to NVIDIA H100 datasheet [https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/h100/].
GPU memory per chip: value=80000000000.0 unit=bytes dist=point - sourced to NVIDIA H100 80GB [https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/h100/].
Bytes per parameter (FP8): value=1 unit=bytes dist=point - sourced to FP8 convention.
DeepSeek V4 total parameters: value=1600000000000.0 unit=params dist=point - sourced to OpenRouter / Morph [https://www.morphllm.com/deepseek-v4].
DeepSeek V4 active parameters: value=37000000000.0 unit=params dist=point - sourced to DeepSeek V3/V4 architecture [https://www.morphllm.com/deepseek-v4].
Memory overhead factor: value=1.2 unit=x dist=triangular - sourced to Engineering estimate.
2031 frontier model total params: value=12800000000000.0 unit=params dist=triangular - sourced to Assumption (~8x 1.6T).
Single-stream output speed (OpenRouter, NovitaAI): value=66 unit=tok/s dist=triangular - sourced to OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 providers [https://openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro].
Effective parallel streams per inference GPU: value=12 unit=streams dist=triangular - sourced to Derived from data points (see note) [https://epoch.ai/gradient-updates/is-a-compute-crunch-coming].
Input price (first-party): value=0.435 unit=$/Mtok dist=point - sourced to DeepSeek API [https://www.morphllm.com/deepseek-v4].
Output price (first-party): value=0.87 unit=$/Mtok dist=point - sourced to DeepSeek API [https://www.morphllm.com/deepseek-v4].
Input price (3rd-party median): value=1.6 unit=$/Mtok dist=triangular - sourced to OpenRouter providers [https://openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro].
Output price (3rd-party median): value=3.17 unit=$/Mtok dist=triangular - sourced to OpenRouter providers [https://openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro].
Input : output token ratio: value=3.0 unit=x dist=triangular - sourced to Typical workload.
Fraction of 2031 fleet doing inference: value=0.35 unit=frac dist=triangular - sourced to Epoch (~44% today); user range 20-50% [https://epoch.ai/data/ai-companies].
Anthropic 2026 inference share: value=0.5 unit=frac dist=triangular - sourced to Epoch (~44% OpenAI) [https://epoch.ai/data/ai-companies].
Anthropic ARR: value=45000000000.0 unit=$/yr dist=triangular - sourced to Willison / SemiAnalysis [https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/29/anthropic/].
OpenAI ARR: value=25000000000.0 unit=$/yr dist=triangular - sourced to Sacra [https://sacra.com/c/openai/].
Other LLM revenue (Google/MSFT/xAI...): value=30000000000.0 unit=$/yr dist=triangular - sourced to Estimate.
Anthropic total compute fleet: value=2000000.0 unit=H100e dist=triangular - sourced to Epoch chip owners [https://epoch.ai/data/ai-chip-owners].
Global compute stock 2026: value=20000000.0 unit=H100e dist=uniform - sourced to Epoch (~16.5M end-2025) [https://epoch.ai/data/ai-chip-sales].
Compute growth rate: value=3.0 unit=x/yr dist=triangular - sourced to Epoch 3.3x; Dario ~3x [https://epoch.ai/data-insights/ai-chip-production].
Projection horizon: value=5 unit=yr dist=point - sourced to 2026->2031.
Token capacity 2031 (full-fleet inference): value=1000000000000.0 unit=tok/s dist=lognormal - sourced to Epoch compute crunch [https://epoch.ai/gradient-updates/is-a-compute-crunch-coming].
Total internet size: value=500000000000000.0 unit=tokens dist=point - sourced to EducatingSilicon [https://www.educatingsilicon.com/2024/05/09/how-much-llm-training-data-is-there-in-the-limit/].

White-collar workers 2031: value=1500000000.0 unit=workers dist=uniform - sourced to Forrester ~1.25B (2018) [https://www.forrester.com/blogs/the-global-information-worker-population-swells-to-1-25-billion-in-2018].
Human cognition rate: value=1000 unit=tok/min dist=triangular - sourced to Epoch ~500 (380 wpm) [https://epoch.ai/gradient-updates/how-many-digital-workers-could-openai-deploy].
Global human wages: value=50000000000000.0 unit=$/yr dist=point - sourced to Dario (Dwarkesh) [https://www.dwarkesh.com/p/dario-amodei-2].
Seconds per year: value=31536000 unit=s dist=point - sourced to 365*24*3600.
model.py reads parameters.csv only - zero hardcoded numbers in code (single source of truth).
Implemented one sampler covering point/uniform/triangular/lognormal; lognormal parameterised from P5/P95.
Each method is a documented pure function (deepseek, macro, model, epoch).
Added hand-check assertions: GPUs/copy=24 & 192, macro rev/GPU=$5k, Anthropic rev/model~$1.08M - all pass.
Derived rev_per_gpu_macro = industry revenue / total stock (not a hardcode).
Derived rev_per_gpu_deepseek from single-stream tps x effective concurrency x seconds x output price.
Derived Anthropic rev/model from ARR / (inference fleet / GPUs-per-copy) - empirical, no decline factor.
copies_2031 derived = stock_2031 x inference_fraction / GPUs-per-copy-2031 (was hardcoded before).
gpus_per_copy derived from model size, bytes/param, overhead, GPU memory (both 2026 and 2031).
Confirmed DeepSeek/per-GPU method runs hot (~$135T, > world GDP) - flagged as non-credible upper bound, not headline.
Defined Pooled estimate as model-based + Epoch (the two credible methods); excludes the hot per-GPU & macro methods.
Verified GPU-DeepSeek and Token-OpenRouter produce identical numbers - presented once to remove redundancy.
report.qmd renders entirely from parameters.csv + model.py; verified all Python chunks execute.
Added a 'How to edit this simulation' callout pointing to parameters.csv with the distribution legend.
Raw-data table preserves a clickable source link for every parameter.
Derived-quantities table includes a 'How' column showing each formula in words.
Documented all four methods in prose, including why GPU and token framings are identical.
Results table shows central + P5/P50/P95 per method and a bolded pooled row.
Method-comparison chart: log x-axis, median dots + P5-P95 bars, DeepSeek in red as upper bound, doc-$2T reference.
Pooled-distribution chart: median, mean and P5-P95 band marked; clipped tail for legibility.
Kept charts to a minimal, elegant pair (per 'minimal handful').
Scale & human table reframed as a capacity statement, not a jobs claim (Epoch caveat).
Caveats section flags the hot per-GPU method, the no-deflation choice, and the swing parameters.
Set embed-resources:true so the rendered HTML is a single portable file; df-print:kable for clean tables.
Built a render_preview.py twin so the report is viewable without a Quarto install; identical logic via model.py.
Report, preview HTML and Excel all source the SAME parameters.csv + model.py - no divergence possible.
Rebuilt Excel from parameters.csv: 3 sheets (Cover/Raw data/Results) down from 8 - merged Inputs summary into Raw data.
Derived quantities are live formulas on Raw data linking to parameter cells (no hardcoded derived values).
Every parameter row carries a clickable source hyperlink and a note.
Deleted the 'Rev/chip decline' parameter and all references to it.
Four methods are live formulas referencing Raw data; verified they equal the Python engine to the dollar (134.7/24.3/9.6/35.0T).
Monte-Carlo summary kept as a clearly-labelled static snapshot, pointing to report.qmd for the live MC (avoids re-introducing a 50k-formula sheet).
Embedded the method-comparison chart on Results.
Applied blue=input / black=formula / green=link colour convention consistently.
Cover sheet states how to edit and where the live MC lives.
recalc.py: zero formula errors.
Cross-checked Excel derived values vs model.py (GPUs 24/192, macro $5k, Anthropic $1.08M, DeepSeek/GPU $79k) - all match.
inference fraction parameter is 20-50% (Triangular 0.20/0.35/0.50) as requested, used by 3 of 4 methods.
Review: lognormal 'value' didn't match its sampled median - inconsistent central vs MC.
Review: single_stream_tps triangular near-degenerate (mode 66 vs max 68) - skewed mean low.
Review: m_deepseek priced output tokens only - input revenue dropped vs the blended model.
Review: blended_fp / first-party prices computed but unused (dead derived quantity).
Review: ds_active_params loaded but never used.
Review: growth 3.0 contradicts note citing Epoch 3.3x (60% swing over 5 yrs).
Review: workers uniform mean (1.3B) != stated central (1.5B).
Review: anthropic_inf_share value 0.5 vs cited 44%.
Review: report claimed 'nothing hardcoded' while several literals existed.
Review: 'four independent ways' overstated independence (shared price/seconds drivers).
Review: $2T reference line on chart unlabelled.
Review: derived 'How' column restated CSV values as literals (desync risk).
Review: no load-time validation that low<=value<=high.
Review: dist semantics (which fields each uses) not documented in the CSV/editor.
Review: human-comparison table presented token ratio as clean multiple (mislead risk).
Review: __main__ assertions would fire spuriously after a user edits the CSV.
Review: pooled selector reused the sample seed (selector correlated with draws).
Review: model-based method assumes revenue-per-copy invariant to 8x larger 2031 model.
Review: 1e6 Mtok scaling appeared as a bare literal.
Review: H100e unit undefined for a non-expert.
Fixed: lognormal now uses value as the MEDIAN (mu=ln(value)); central run matches MC median.
Fixed: re-centred single_stream_tps triangular to (45,62,68) - less degenerate, mean nearer mode.
Documented: m_deepseek is output-only by design (input revenue excluded); per-GPU method is an upper bound, so no inflation added.
Fixed: removed dead blended_fp from derive(); kept first-party prices in CSV but labelled 'reference only'.
Fixed: ds_active_params now feeds a derived FLOP throughput cross-check (peak_tps_flop) - no longer dead.
Addressed: kept growth central 3.0 but rewrote note to state it is deliberately conservative vs Epoch 3.3 (in range).
Fixed: workers_2031 switched uniform->triangular so 1.5B is the mode.
Fixed: anthropic_inf_share value 0.5->0.44 to match the cited Epoch figure; note reconciled.
Fixed: softened the report claim to 'no headline figures hardcoded' and interpolated the prose literals.
Fixed: relabelled 'four framings (not statistically independent)'; stated why model+Epoch are pooled.
Fixed: added a labelled annotation to the $2T reference line.
Fixed: derived 'How' column now f-strings interpolated from parameters (auto-updates with CSV).
Fixed: load_params now asserts low<=value<=high for every non-point row.
Fixed: documented dist-field semantics in the report callout and README.
Fixed: added inline 'capacity, NOT cognitive-output equivalence' caveat to the human-comparison row.
Fixed: moved checks into selftest() that print ok/CHANGED instead of hard-asserting - safe after edits.
Fixed: pooled selector now uses an independent RNG (seed+1).
Fixed: added explicit stationarity-assumption note to the model-based method.
Fixed: introduced named TOKENS_PER_M constant for the $/Mtok conversion.
Fixed: added H100e glossary note to the first H100e parameter row.
Fixed: audit caught a residual '$100B ÷ 20M' literal in the methods prose - generalised it.

Confirmed Excel and Python agree to <0.5% on all four methods and key derived quantities.
